Bullous dermatitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the skin characterized by the presence of bullae which are filled with fluid.
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 2: Blisters covering 10 - 30% BSA; painful blisters; limiting instrumental ADL
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 3: Blisters covering >30% BSA; limiting self care ADL
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 4: Blisters covering >30% BSA; associated with fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated

Erythema multiforme.Definition: A disorder characterized by target lesions (a pink-red ring around a pale center).
Erythema multiforme.Grade 1: Target lesions covering <10% BSA and not associated with skin tenderness
Erythema multiforme.Grade 2: Target lesions covering 10 - 30% BSA and associated with skin tenderness
Erythema multiforme.Grade 3: Target lesions covering >30% BSA and associated with oral or genital erosions
Erythema multiforme.Grade 4: Target lesions covering >30% BSA; associated with fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated

Erythroderma.Definition: A disorder characterized by generalized inflammatory erythema and exfoliation. The inflammatory process involves > 90% of the body surface area.
Erythroderma.Grade 2: Erythema covering >90% BSA without associated symptoms; limiting instrumental ADL
Erythroderma.Grade 3: Erythema covering >90% BSA with associated symptoms (e.g., pruritus or tenderness); limiting self care ADL
Erythroderma.Grade 4: Erythema covering >90% BSA with associated fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated

Rash maculo-papular.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of macules (flat) and papules (elevated). Also known as morbillform rash, it is one of the most common cutaneous adverse events, frequently affecting the upper trunk, spreading centripetally and associated with pruritis.
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 1: Macules/papules covering <10% BSA with or without symptoms (e.g., pruritus, burning, tightness)
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 2: Macules/papules covering 10 - 30% BSA with or without symptoms (e.g., pruritus, burning, tightness); limiting instrumental ADL; rash covering > 30% BSA with or without mild symptoms
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 3: Macules/papules covering >30% BSA with moderate or severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL

Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by less than 10% total body skin area separation of dermis. The syndrome is thought to be a hypersensitivity complex affecting the skin and the mucous membranes.
Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Grade 3: Skin sloughing covering <10% BSA with associated signs (e.g., erythema, purpura, epidermal detachment, and mucous membrane detachment)
Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Grade 4: Skin sloughing covering 10 - 30% BSA with associated signs (e.g., erythema, purpura, epidermal detachment and mucous membrane detachment)

Toxic epidermal necrolysis.Definition: A disorder characterized by greater than 30% total body skin area separation of dermis. The syndrome is thought to be a hypersensitivity complex affecting the skin and the mucous membranes.
Toxic epidermal necrolysis.Grade 4: Skin sloughing covering >=30% BSA with associated symptoms (e.g., erythema, purpura, or epidermal detachment)
